title: Tainia ponggolensis
taxonomy: Tainia ponggolensis is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which includes orchids. This plant is further classified as a genus within the subtribe Malaxideinae. The subfamily is part of the tribe Malaxideae, which consists of diverse orchid species. Tainia ponggolensis is native to Southeast Asia and is also known as the Ponggol orchid. Further classification would include the species being part of a specific section within its genus.
uses: Tainia ponggolensis is a species of orchid, and while specific information on its medicinal uses is limited, it is often researched for its potential in traditional medicine. The plant is native to Singapore and Malaysia, and its uses in these regions are primarily based on local knowledge and folklore.
